note:
在柏林的一个旧机场里，在9天的时间里挤出6场比赛，这并不是任何人预期到的E&39；方程式赛车赛季会结束的样子。不过，电动赛车系列做到了这一点。今年2月，该系列赛在摩洛哥举行的第五轮世界巡演结束后，暂停了世界巡演。当这个停顿发生时，冠军已经相当接近了，安东尼奥·费利克斯·达·科斯塔在为DS Techeetah球队获得第一场胜利后领先。
当两支球队从赛前的隔离中走出来时，达科斯塔在状态上没有任何损失。他在第6场和第7场比赛中不败，这两场比赛都是在1.5英里(2.35公里)赛道的反向版本上举行的，E方程式在之前所有访问坦佩尔霍夫机场的比赛中都使用了这条赛道。与此同时，他的竞争对手中没有一人能够始终如一地完成比赛，保持在喊出的距离内。
在第8场和第9场比赛中，E方程式将所有10个转弯都保持在相同的位置，但系列赛回到了围绕坦佩尔霍夫战前混凝土的正常比赛方向。宝马I Andretti的Maxi Guenther以微弱优势击败enVision Virgin的Robert Frjins赢得了第8场比赛，达科斯塔位居第四，仅次于他的队友和两届冠军让-埃里克·韦尔涅(Jean-Eric Vergne)。然而，这第四名足以使达科斯塔在锦标赛中的领先地位无懈可击。第二天，Guenther在第9场比赛中从英雄变成了零，他在第一圈撞车。这场比赛见证了DS Techeetah赛车和两辆日产之间的激烈竞争，Vergne击败了达科斯塔和日产的Sebastian Buemi赢得了胜利。
在最后两场比赛-第10轮和第11轮-系列赛移动了一些障碍，并将坦佩尔霍夫重新配置为16圈，1.6英里(2.5公里)的配置。更进一步的是，DS Techeetahs、Buemi和奥迪的Lucas di Grassi在第10轮的排位赛中都错过了排位圈的时机，所以这四位冠军不得不从发车区后面开始这场比赛。结果是日产的奥利弗·罗兰(Oliver Rowland)赢得了E方程式赛车的首个胜利。
在柏林的最后一站看到了E方程式的另一个新赢家：斯托菲尔·范霍恩夺得了他和梅赛德斯-奔驰在系列赛中的第一场胜利。范霍恩紧随其后的是他的队友尼克·德弗里斯(Nyck De Vries)，布埃米位居第三。范霍恩在今年的锦标赛中获得亚军，但对于像赛车手这样竞争激烈的人来说，这可能是对达科斯塔158分的87分的微弱安慰。DS Techeetah赢得了车队冠军，这是他们六个赛季以来在方程式赛车中的第二个赛季。
这就是现在全电动赛车系列的总结。如果一切按计划进行--我知道这不再是板上钉钉的事--第七季将于2021年1月中旬在智利圣地亚哥开播。
note_en:
Squeezing  six races out an old airport in Berlin over the course of nine days wasn't how anyone expected Formula E's season to end. The electric racing series made it work, though. The series pressed pause on its world tour after round five, which was held in Morocco in February. When that pause happened, the championship was fairly close, with Antonio Felix da Costa leading the way after getting his first win for the DS Techeetah team.
  As the teams emerged from their pre-race quarantine, da Costa had lost nothing in terms of form. He was unbeatable in races 6 and 7, which were held on a reversed version of the 1.5-mile (2.35km) course that Formula E has used for all its previous visits to the Tempelhof airport. Meanwhile, none of his rivals were able to finish consistently enough to stay within shouting distance.
  For races 8 and 9, Formula E kept all ten turns in the same place, but the series returned to its normal racing direction around Tempelhof's prewar concrete. BMW i Andretti's Maxi Guenther narrowly beat Envision Virgin's Robert Frjins to win race 8, with da Costa in fourth place behind his teammate and double-champion Jean-Eric Vergne. However, this fourth place was enough to make da Costa's championship lead unassailable. The following day, Guenther went from hero to zero in race 9 when he crashed out on lap 1. This race saw a pretty close fight between both DS Techeetah cars and the pair of Nissans with Vergne beating da Costa and Nissan's Sebastian Buemi for the win.
  For the final two races—rounds 10 and 11—the series moved some barriers and reconfigured Tempelhof into a 16-turn, 1.6-mile (2.5km) configuration. Shaking things up further, for round 10 DS Techeetahs, Buemi, and Audi's Lucas di Grassi all mistimed their qualifying laps, and so the four champions had to start that race from the back of the grid. The result was a maiden Formula E win for Nissan's Oliver Rowland.
 The final outing in Berlin saw another new winner in Formula E: Stoffel Vandoorne took his and Mercedes-Benz's first victories in the series. Vandoorne was followed to the flag by his teammate Nyck de Vries, with Buemi in third. Vandoorne ended the year runner up in the championship, but with 87 points to da Costa's 158 that's probably slim consolation for one as competitive as a racing driver. DS Techeetah won the teams championship, their second in Formula E's six seasons.
 That's now a wrap from the all-electric racing series. If things go to plan—and that's no longer a sure thing, I know—season seven should start in Santiago, Chile in mid-January 2021.
title: 第六季在柏林结束，E方程式加冕新冠军
title_en: Formula E crowns a new champion as season six ends in Berlin

去年，Facebook宣布了一项合并其三大消息网络的计划：有朝一日WhatsApp、Facebook Messenger和Instagram Direct都将成为同一项服务。周五晚些时候，The Verge发现了迈向Facebook统一消息乌托邦的第一步--Facebook Messenger正在接管Instagram消息。
周五，Instagram应用程序开始弹出一条消息，上面写着：Instagram上有了一种新的发信息方式。除了Instagram和Facebook用户之间的交叉交流之外，这一更新还承诺将有一个五颜六色的新外观，表情符号反应，以及滑动回复消息的能力。当您更新时，Instagram的消息图标将更改为Facebook Messenger徽标。这一更新似乎正在iOS和Android中推出，但目前，过渡是可选的。
在给The Verge的一份声明中，Facebook表示，“一小部分人能够更新到Instagram消息的新测试体验。我们希望他们喜欢这一经验，我们期待着在其他国家进行测试，这样我们就可以继续从中学习。“。据传闻，这项服务的推出似乎比一小群人大得多，但我们不得不相信Facebook的说法。
Facebook是通讯领域的主导者。根据Statista的数据，世界上最大的即时通讯应用是Facebook的WhatsApp，月活跃用户约20亿。Facebook Messenger是Statista排行榜的第二大服务，拥有13亿用户，紧随其后的是中国的微信应用，拥有12亿用户。
这份榜单不包括苹果的iMessage，后者应该跻身全球前四大即时通讯应用之列。苹果公司表示，2019年有9亿活跃iPhone用户，活跃的苹果设备总数为14亿。用户通常拥有不止一台苹果设备，因此声称每台设备都是唯一的用户是不正确的。IMessage是默认的iOS短信服务，因此iMessage的用户基数可能接近iPhone用户总数。Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格似乎肯定尊重苹果的即时通讯游戏，称iMessage是Facebook在2018年即时通讯领域遥遥领先的最大竞争对手。
Facebook的下一个重大举措将是合并WhatsApp和Facebook Messenger，这将创建一个33亿用户的超级网络，约占全球单一即时通讯服务人口的43%。
note_en:
Last year,  Facebook announced a plan to merge its three big messaging networks: someday WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, and Instagram Direct would all be the same service. Late Friday,  The Verge spotted the first move toward Facebook's unified messaging utopia— Facebook Messenger is taking over Instagram messaging.
 On Friday, the Instagram app started popping up a message saying, "There's a new way to message on Instagram." Besides cross-communication between Instagram and Facebook users, the update promises a "new colorful look," emoji reactions, and the ability to swipe to reply to messages. Instagram's messaging icon changes to a Facebook Messenger logo when you update. The update appears to be rolling out across iOS and Android, and for now, the transition is optional.
 In a statement to The Verge, Facebook said, “A small set of people were able to update to a new test experience for Instagram messaging. We hope they enjoy the experience and we are looking forward to testing it in other countries so we can keep learning from this.” Anecdotally this rollout seems a lot bigger than "a small set of people," but we'll have to take Facebook's word for it.
 Facebook is the dominant player in the messaging space. The world's biggest messaging app is Facebook's WhatsApp, with around two billion monthly active users, according to  Statista. Facebook Messenger is the second biggest service in Statista's list, with 1.3 billion users, followed by China's WeChat app, with 1.2 billion users.
 That list doesn't include Apple's iMessage, which should be somewhere in the top four messaging apps worldwide. In 2019,  Apple said there were 900 million active iPhone users out there, and 1.4 billion active Apple devices total. Users often have more than one Apple device, so it's not right to claim each device is one unique user. iMessage is the default iOS texting service, so the iMessage user base is probably close to the total number of iPhone users. Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg certainly seems to respect Apple's messaging game, calling iMessage Facebook's " biggest competitor by far" in the message space in 2018.
 The next big move for Facebook will be merging WhatsApp and Facebook Messenger, which would create a 3.3 billion user super network, accounting for about 43 percent of the world's population on a single messaging service.
title: Facebook Messenger开始接管Instagram Direct Messages
title_en: Facebook Messenger starts taking over Instagram Direct messages

但今天，这一未来又近了一步，有消息称，根据真人创造人工智能驱动的合成人物的Hour One公司完成了由Galaxy Interactive(通过其Galaxy EOS VC Fund)、Remagine Ventures和Kindred Ventures(Amaranthine参与)牵头的500万美元种子融资。
Hour One将利用这笔资金扩大其人工智能驱动的云平台，搭载“数千”个新角色，并扩大其商业活动。成立于2019年的Hour One开发基于真人创造高质量数字角色的技术。这个想法是以一种高度可伸缩性和成本效益的方式生成基于视频的生产级角色。这样做的结果是，看起来像是真人的人可以谈论任何产品或主题，甚至可以无限规模地谈论。
这在2020年国际消费电子展(CES 2020)的“真实或合成”相似性测试中得到了展示，挑战人们区分由其人工智能生成的真实字符和合成字符。
Hour One的创始人兼首席执行官奥伦·阿哈伦在一份声明中说：“我们相信真人的人造角色将成为我们日常生活的一部分。我们的愿景是，第一小时将推动合成字符的使用，以提高跨市场和用例的企业和人员之间的沟通质量。通过让每个人都能与我们可扩展的云平台一起创造自己的角色，我们将为下一代远程企业对人的互动提供多样化的解决方案。“一小时一小时”目前正在与电商、教育、汽车、通信和企业部门的公司合作，预计整个2020年都会有更多的行业应用。
该公司还在CES 2020上展示了其“真实或合成”的肖像测试，挑战人们区分由其人工智能生成的真实字符和合成字符。
然而，真正的问题是如何在不被滥用的情况下部署这项技术。
联合创始人兼首席技术官利奥尔·哈基姆(Lior Hakim)表示，这一潜在问题通过加密技术得到解决，以确保角色的使用和权利，使“任何人都能识别我们的视频，并将其标记为已更改，以通知观众”。该公司还表示，它对如何使用其技术有道德政策守则。
银河互动的联合创始人兼董事总经理萨姆·恩格尔巴特表示，这家初创公司“道德驱动的合成视频创作方式”是关键，“考虑到新冠肺炎给实况演员制作带来的挑战，现在是各种规模的企业用”一小时“合成人物制作内容的绝佳时机。”
Remagine Ventures的联合创始人兼执行合伙人埃兹·维德拉(Eze Vidra)表示，显然，这将降低合成人物创作的成本，这意味着任何文本内容都可以“自动转换为一个人的真人视频，通过说出文本来吸引观众”。
“一小时”商业战略负责人娜塔莉·蒙比奥特在接受TechCrunch采访时表示，该公司有一种独特的能力，可以“基本上让任何人登上飞机，把他们变成一个人造角色，逼真地复制这个人。”所以它不是一个化身，也不是那个人的一个版本。它的长相和行为真的很像那个人。然后，您基本上可以通过上传新文本来生成新内容。例如，在电子商务中，你可以选择你的角色，让他们展示你的产品或做产品展示。这意味着每个产品SKU都可以有自己的视频演示。“
note_en:
But that future comes a step closer today with the news that  Hour One, which creates AI-driven synthetic characters based on real humans, closes a $5 million seed funding led by  Galaxy Interactive (via its  Galaxy EOS VC Fund),  Remagine Ventures and  Kindred Ventures (with participation of  Amaranthine).
 Hour One will use the funds to scale its AI-driven cloud platform, onboard ‘thousands’ of new characters, and expand its commercial activities.   Founded in 2019, Hour One develops technologies for creating high-quality digital characters based on real people. The idea is to generate production-grade video-based characters in a highly scalable and cost-effective way. The upshot of this is that what appears to be a real human could talk about any product or subject at all, to the point of infinite scale.
  This was showcased at its “real or synthetic”  likeness test at CES 2020, challenging people to distinguish between real and synthetic characters generated by its AI.
 Oren Aharon, Hour One’s Founder and CEO said in a statement: “We believe that synthetic characters of real people will become a part of our everyday life. Our vision is that Hour One will drive the use of synthetic characters to improve the quality of communication between businesses and people across markets and use cases. By enabling each person to create their own character together with our scalable cloud platform, we will provide a variety of solutions for next-gen remote business-to-human interactions.”   Hour One is currently working with companies in the e-commerce, education, automotive, communication, and enterprise sectors, with expanded industry applications expected throughout 2020.
 The company also showcased its “real or synthetic” likeness test at CES 2020, challenging people to distinguish between real and synthetic characters generated by its AI.
 The real issue, however, is how will this technology be deployed without it being abused.
 Lior Hakim, cofounder and CTO says this potential problem is dealt with via encryption technologies to secure the use and rights of the characters enabling “anyone to identify our videos as well as mark them as altered to notify the viewers”. The company also says it has an ethical policy code for how its technology is used.
 Sam Englebardt, Co-Founder and Managing Director of Galaxy Interactive says the startup’s “ethics-driven approach to the creation of synthetic video” is key and that “given how challenging production with live actors has become as a result of COVID-19, now is the perfect time for businesses of all sizes to produce their content with Hour One’s synthetic characters.”
 Clearly this will reduce the cost of synthetic character creation meaning any textual content could be “automatically translated into a live-action video of a person that engages an audience by speaking the text” said  Eze Vidra,   Co-Founder and Managing Partner at  Remagine Ventures  .
 Speaking to  TechCrunch,   Business strategy lead for Hour One Natalie Monbiot, said the company has a unique ability to onboard “basically any human being and turn them into a synthetic character that’s a lifelike replica of that person. So it’s not an avatar or a version of that person. It really does look and behave like that person. You can then basically generate new content by uploading new texts. So, for example, in e-commerce, you can pick your characters and get them to present your product or do a product presentation. This means every single product SKU can have its own video presentation.”
title: 第一小时筹集500万美元种子，从真人中生成人工智能驱动的合成角色
title_en: Hour One raises $5M Seed to generate AI-driven synthetic characters from real humans

note:
布莱恩·苏格(Brian Sugar)与妻子丽莎·苏格(Lisa Sugar)共同创立了女性生活方式同名网站。收购后，他成为第九集团(Group Nine)整个公司的总裁(该集团还拥有Thrillist，NoowThis，The Dodo和Seeker)，并加入了公司董事会。
那份工作看起来可能与他去年秋天预期的大不相同。早在4月份，该公司就不得不裁员7%，Sugar称这是“我职业生涯中最糟糕的日子之一”。与此同时，他仍然对在线广告业务充满信心。在他看来，是电视广告在脸上遭受了“巨大的打击”，而且永远不会恢复。
“我们喜欢认为自己是最快、最具创新精神的出版商之一，”Sugar告诉我。“现在是我们炫耀的时候了。”
你可以阅读下面经过编辑、更新和浓缩的我们的谈话记录，我在其中与Sugar谈论了他的角色是如何演变的，他如何在困难时期激励团队，以及在转向远程工作的过程中失去了什么。
TechCrunch：显然，自从我们上次交谈以来，已经过去了疯狂的几个月。你现在的工作是什么样子的？
布莱恩·苏格：嗯，我感觉自己就像一个数据挖掘者，在寻找答案。我感觉自己像个黑客马拉松工程师。我觉得自己像个心理医生。你知道，我们喜欢认为自己是最快、最具创新精神的出版商之一。现在是我们炫耀的时候了。
(我们刚刚)查看了人们如何跨平台消费我们内容的数据。在我们的网站上，我们提出了一些非常有趣的想法，这些想法我们已经实现了。我们在周五举办了这些非常酷的黑客马拉松来快速构建东西，因为很多人觉得他们有更多的时间-因为你不必出差去开会，你可以完成更多的工作。有些人觉得他们更有效率。
我们非常乐观。我们所有的品牌都非常乐观，(整个)公司也是如此。
您提到推出一些新产品来应对观众行为的变化。有没有什么例子？
第一个是PopSugar健身这件事。我们计划在5月份推出付费健身订阅服务，但(3月份)每个人都在家里工作，我们决定尽可能快地推出这项服务。我们在接下来的那个周末推出了它。自从3月下旬发布以来，在过去的几个月里，我们已经有20万人注册，我们在上面有5万月度活跃用户。
note_en:
Brian Sugar   founded the eponymous women’s lifestyle site with his wife  Lisa Sugar  . Post-acquisition, he’s become president for the entirety of Group Nine (which also owns Thrillist, NowThis, The Dodo and Seeker) and also joined the company’s board.
 That job probably looks very different from what he expected last fall. The company had to  lay off 7% of its staff back in April, which Sugar described as “one of the worst days of my career.” At the same time, he remains confident about the online advertising business. In his view, it’s TV advertising that’s taken a “huge punch” in the face and will never recover.
 “We like to think of ourselves as one of the fastest, most innovative publishers out there,” Sugar told me. “And now’s the time for us to kind of show that off.”
 You can read an edited, updated and condensed transcript of our conversation below, in which I talked to Sugar about how his role has evolved, how he motivates the team during difficult times and what gets lost in the shift to remote work.
 TechCrunch: Obviously, it’s been a crazy couple of months since we last talked. What does your job look like now?
 Brian Sugar: Well, I feel like a data miner, searching for answers. I feel like a hackathon engineer. And I feel like a therapist. You know, we like to think of ourselves as one of the fastest, most innovative publishers out there. And now’s the time for us to kind of show that off.
 [We’ve just been] looking at data on how people are consuming our content across platforms. And on our site, we’ve come up with some really interesting ideas that we’ve implemented. We’ve been having these really cool hackathon Fridays to build stuff quickly, because a lot of people feel like they have a little bit more time on their hands — because you don’t have to travel to meetings, you can get more work done. Some people feel they’re more efficient.
 We’re extremely optimistic. All our brands are extremely optimistic, and so is [the whole] company.
 You mentioned launching some new products to respond to how audience behavior is changing. Are there any examples?
 The first one [is] the PopSugar Fitness thing. We were planning on launching a paid workout subscription service in May, but everybody was working from home [in March], and we decided to pull the launch all the way up to as fast as we can launch it. We launched it that following weekend. Since the launch in late March, over the past few months, we’ve had 200,000 people sign up, and we have 50,000 monthly active users on it.
title: PopSugar联合创始人表示，流行病将为数字媒体创造“一笔巨大的意外之财”
title_en: PopSugar co-founder says pandemic will create ‘a huge windfall’ for digital media

note:
该计划旨在补充苹果现有的由5000多家苹果授权服务提供商组成的网络，如百思买(Best Buy)，它们同时处理保修期内和保修期外的维修。从某种程度上说，该计划源于消费者的需求。许多iPhone用户出于各种原因转向未经授权的维修店-例如，可能这家店离他们的家更近，可以更快地修复他们的设备，或者提供更实惠的维修。但这一选择可能会导致消费者体验参差不齐，因为商店被拒之门外，不能使用苹果的官方零部件。
自在美国推出以来，独立维修店计划已扩展到140多家企业和700多个新地点。今年夏天，苹果宣布该计划现在也将扩展到国际上，包括欧洲和加拿大。
然而，到目前为止，该计划只专注于iPhone的维修，而不是Mac。展望未来，这些维修店和其他有资格的公司也将能够获得苹果正版工具、维修手册、诊断程序、官方部件和其他资源，这些资源也是他们在Mac电脑上进行常见的保修期外维修所需的。苹果公司表示，该计划是免费注册的，维修培训也是免费的。
路透社最先报道了该计划扩大的消息。苹果还向TechCrunch证实了这一细节，并指出该公司认为最安全、最可靠的维修是由训练有素的技术人员使用苹果官方部件进行的。该公司还表示，它希望消费者对他们的维修工作做得正确感到有信心。
该计划扩大的消息来得正是时候，因为苹果对消费者“维修”自己设备的权利的立场，是美国众议院反垄断委员会正在调查的众多议题之一.。
该小组委员会上个月举行了一次听证会，询问苹果首席执行官蒂姆·库克在一系列问题上的立场，比如App Store和佣金结构。虽然不是国会听证会本身的主要焦点，但作为小组委员会对苹果调查的一部分而收集的文件包括一些内部电子邮件，其中显示了该公司对其修复计划和修复立法的权利存在怎样的冲突，而苹果多年来一直在游说反对这一点。
文件显示，在一封电子邮件中，苹果高管考虑告诉记者其当时即将推出的正版部件维修计划，以表明其对更有利于消费者的维修政策的承诺。在另一封电子邮件中，苹果高管讨论了维修手册是如何在未经许可的情况下发布的，这表明苹果的维修政策缺乏连贯的战略。
通过进一步扩大其独立维修计划，现在将Mac包括在内，苹果不仅受益于通过扩大获得正版部件来更好地为客户服务，而且还受益于将反垄断调查的重点从这个特定的话题上转移，至少如果不是其他话题的话。
note_en:
The program was meant to complement Apple’s existing network of over 5,000 Apple Authorized Service Providers, like Best Buy, which handle both in- and out-of-warranty repairs. To some extent, the program arose from consumer demand. Many iPhone users were turning to unauthorized repair shops for a variety of reasons — perhaps the shop was closer to their home, could fix their device more quickly, or offered more affordable repairs, for example. But this choice could result in an uneven consumer experience as the shops were locked out from using official Apple parts.
 Since its U.S. launch, the independent repair shop program expanded to over 140 businesses and over 700 new locations. This summer, Apple announced the program would now expand internationally as well, to both Europe and Canada.
  To date, however, the program was only focused on iPhone repairs — not Mac. Going forward, these repair shops and others that qualify will be able to access Apple-genuine tools, repair manuals, diagnostics, official parts, and other resources they need to perform common out-of-warranty repairs on Macs, too. The program is free to sign up for and the repair training is also free, Apple says.
 Reuters first reported the news of the program’s expansion. Apple also confirmed the details to TechCrunch, noting that the company believes the safest and most reliable repair is one that’s handled by a trained technician using official Apple parts. The company said, too, it wants consumers to feel confident that their repairs are being done correctly.
 The news of the program’s expansion is timely, given that Apple’s stance on consumers’ “right to repair” their own devices is one of the many topics under investigation by the U.S. House Antitrust Subcommittee.
 The subcommittee  had last month held a hearing where it asked Apple CEO Tim Cook about his company’s position on a variety of matters, like its App Store and commission structure, for example. Though not a major focus of the Congressional hearing itself, the documents collected as part of the subcommittee’s investigation into Apple included internal emails that showed how the company was conflicted about its repair program and the Right to Repair legislation, which Apple had lobbied against for years.
 In one email, Apple execs weighed telling a reporter about its then-forthcoming Genuine Parts Repair program to demonstrate its commitment to more consumer-friendly repair policies, the documents revealed, In others, Apple execs discussed how repair manuals had been published without clearance, indicating a lack of a cohesive strategy around its approach to repair policies.
 By further expanding its independent repair program to now include the Mac, Apple benefits from not only better serving customers by expanding access to genuine parts, but also from redirecting the focus of the antitrust investigation away from this particular topic, at least, if not the others.
title: 苹果将其独立维修计划扩大至Mac，此前美国反垄断调查审查了该公司的维修政策
title_en: Apple expands its independent repair program to Mac, after US antitrust investigation examined company’s repair policies

美国邮政服务(USPS)的工作人员表示，最近任命的邮政署署长和唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的主要捐赠者路易斯·德乔伊(Louis DeJoy)正在实施的改革造成了重大延误，邮递员难以跟上。
DeJoy是在6月15日被任命的，自那以后，他在USPS做出了有争议的改革，以削减成本，批评人士认为，这是在美国总统寻求11月连任之际削弱该机构的策略。
特朗普本人承认，他希望让邮政服务缺乏资金，这样邮寄投票就会变得困难，因为在冠状病毒大流行期间，预计将有数千万美国人通过邮寄投票。包括巴拉克·奥巴马(Barack Obama)和众议院议长南希·佩洛西(Nancy Pelosi)在内的主要民主党人谴责这些举措是对选举的威胁。
今年7月，DeJoy指示USPS员工，如果邮件延误了他们的航线，并禁止员工加班，就应该留下邮件。8月7日，德乔伊宣布冻结管理层招聘，并计划寻求非工会员工提前退休。
德克萨斯州米德兰的城市邮递员扎克·芬利(Zack Finley)说，“从本质上说，通过命令我们留下邮件，我们被指示违反联邦法律。”芬利是全国邮递员协会(National Association Of Letter Carriers)当地工会商店的管家。对于头等邮件，有联邦法律规定必须在办公室收到邮件的当天投递，否则必须尝试投递。
芬利补充说：“通过遵守违反联邦法律的命令，每个单独的承运人都要为自己的违法行为承担责任。不是每个人都理解这一点，但工会理解。这使航母处于不可能取胜的境地。遵守法律可能会丢掉工作，或者服从命令可能会被逮捕。“。
他还指出，非职业员工向有福利的职业员工的转换已经停止，邮递员在没有足够的时间完成办公室职责的情况下被迫离开路线，让这些任务逐渐堆积起来，造成了进一步的延误。
特朗普8月13日表示，他反对向USPS提供额外资金，以增加邮寄选票的难度。美国邮政总局现在警告说，46个州和华盛顿特区的邮寄选票可能无法按时送达进行统计。
2006年，国会迫使USPS创建了一只720亿美元的基金，以全额支付未来50年员工退休后的医疗费用，这一规定不适用于任何其他联邦机构或公司。自国会采取行动以来，美国邮政总局已经削减了大约20万名员工，以削减成本。在过去的几年里，该基金在USPS内部造成了赤字，在收入下降的情况下，冠状病毒大流行进一步损害了该机构。
美国邮政总局的几名工作人员接受了《卫报》的采访，由于担心报复，他们要求匿名。
弗吉尼亚州里士满地区的一家公司表示，邮递员被迫在上午晚些时候进来，向USPS车站递送邮件的卡车司机也减少了工作时间，导致进行递送和完成工作量的时间减少。邮递员也被迫提前返回车站。
“他们希望我们在上午10点前上街，有些人希望我们下午4点半回来。如果你没有在他们想让你回来的时间里回来，他们会把你写在信上，“邮递员说。“邮件真的被延误了，这让承运商不堪重负。”
邮递员解释说，由于大流行，由于员工要求的标注和休假，USPS的工作人员已经人手不足，最近的变化导致头等邮件出现故障，邮件堆积在车站。邮递员请了两天假，带着四天的邮件回来，试图按常规路线递送。
“这里堆放着一桶桶的邮件，我们只能尽可能多地拿出来。它很重。我在步行路线上。想象一下，你要做一整条街，有50栋房子，胳膊里有60本杂志，还有包裹和包。这很耗时，“他们补充说。
在宾夕法尼亚州费城，一名邮递员解释说，邮件和包裹之所以堆积起来，是因为没有足够的工人像往常一样处理邮件负载。
“我被迫在他们的邮件或他们的邮件是否要寄出去的问题上对顾客撒谎，”邮递员说。
芝加哥郊外的一家邮递员声称，他们的车站发生了邮车未能按时将邮件送到USPS车站的事件，迫使邮递员在几乎没有邮件的情况下出发。
“作为邮政署署长新的”枢轴“计划的一部分，任何没有准备好寄送的东西都会被扣留到第二天。这对很多包裹来说是有问题的，因为如果他们没有及时制造卡车，他们过去只会乘坐晚一点的卡车，但现在他们被推迟到第二天，或者后天，或者后天，如果不能制造卡车，“邮递员说。
“顾客希望我们在他们应该收到包裹的时候拿到他们的包裹，而且肯定不是在包裹应该到达那里的9天之后。”我为我的工作感到自豪，当我不得不递送这样的东西时，我感到羞愧，“承运商补充道。
note_en:
Workers at the United States Postal Service (USPS) say changes being implemented by recently appointed postmaster general and major Donald Trump donor Louis DeJoy are causing major delays, with mail carriers struggling to keep up.
  DeJoy was  appointed on 15 June, and has since made controversial changes at the USPS to cut costs, which critics argue are tactics to undermine the agency as the US president seeks re-election in November.
 Trump himself has admitted to wanting to starve the postal service of funds so that mail-in voting will become difficult, as tens of millions of Americans are expected to vote by mail during the coronavirus pandemic. Leading Democrats, including Barack Obama and the House speaker, Nancy Pelosi, have decried the moves as a threat to the election.
 In July, DeJoy  instructed USPS employees to leave mail behind if it delayed carriers from their routes and prohibited employees from working overtime. On 7 August, DeJoy  announced a management hiring freeze and is  planning to seek early retirements of non-union employees.
  “Essentially, by ordering us to leave behind mail, we are being instructed to break federal law,” said Zack Finley, a city mail carrier in Midland, Texas, and local union shop steward with the National Association of Letter Carriers. “For first-class mail, there is  federal law that says it must be delivered on the day it is received by the office, or have delivery attempted.”
 Finley added: “By following an order to break federal law, each individual carrier assumes liability for their own action that breaks the law. Not everyone understands that, but the union does. It puts the carrier in an impossible to win situation. Obey the law and potentially lose your job or obey the order and possibly be arrested.”
 He also noted conversion of non-career employees to career employees with benefits has been halted, and mail carriers are being forced out on to their routes without being given enough time to complete their office duties, leaving those tasks to gradually pile up, causing further delays.
 Trump  said on 13 August that he opposes providing additional funding to the USPS to make it more difficult to deliver mail-in ballots. The USPS has now  warned that mail ballots in 46 states and Washington DC may not be delivered on time to be counted.
   In 2006, Congress  forced the USPS to create a $72bn fund to fully pay for post-retirement healthcare costs for employees  50 years into the future, which applies to no other federal agency or corporation. Since Congress’ actions, the USPS has  reduced its number of employees by about 200,000 workers to cut costs. The fund  created a deficit within the USPS over the past several years, and the coronavirus pandemic has hurt the agency further amid  revenue declines.
 Several workers at the USPS who spoke to the Guardian requested anonymity for fear of retaliation.
 One in the Richmond, Virginia, area said mail carriers are being forced to come in later in the morning, and truck drivers who deliver mail to USPS stations have also had hours cut, resulting in less time to conduct deliveries and complete workloads. Mail carriers are also being forced to return to stations earlier.
 “They want us out on the street by 10am and some cases want us back at 4.30pm. If you’re not back in the time they want you to return, they write you up,” said the mail carrier. “The mail is really being delayed and it’s overwhelming to the carriers.”
  The mail carrier explained USPS workers are already working understaffed due to callouts and leaves requested by employees due to the pandemic, with the recent changes causing first-class mail to get out of order, and mail to pile up in stations. The carrier took two days off from work, and returned with four days’ worth of mail to try to deliver on their regular route.
 “There are buckets of mail sitting, and we are only able to take out as much as we can. It’s heavy. I’m on a walking route. Just imagine having to do a whole street with 50 houses, you have 60 magazines in your arm, plus packages and your bag. It’s time-consuming,” they added.
 In Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, a mail carrier explained mail and packages are piling up because there aren’t enough workers as usual to handle the mail loads.
 “I’m forced to lie to customers about their mail or if their mail is coming out,” said the carrier.
 A carrier outside of Chicago claimed their station has experienced occurrences where mail trucks failed to deliver post to the USPS station on time, forcing carriers to go out on routes with practically no mail.
 “As part of the postmaster general’s new ‘pivot’ plan, anything that is not ready for dispatch gets held back for the next day. This is problematic for a lot of packages, because if they don’t make the truck in time, they used to just go on a later truck, but now they get held back till the next day, or the next day, or the next day, if it doesn’t make the truck,” said the mail carrier.
 “Customers depend on us to get their parcels when they’re supposed to get them, and certainly not nine days after it is supposed to get there. I take pride in my job, and I’m ashamed when I have to deliver stuff like that,” the carrier added.
title: 美国邮局承运商“被迫减少工作时间”，留下“成桶的邮件”
title_en: US post office carriers "forced to work fewer hours," leaving "buckets of mail"

奇怪的是，我不记得我是什么时候或者是怎么拿到东芝FlashAir卡的。这些支持WiFi的SD卡是用来将照片从数码相机传输到计算机的。
想象一下，当硬件传输速度接近70 Mbps时，有人会使用4 Mbps的无线连接来传输照片，这似乎是荒谬的。
但是，如果您处于无法使用SD卡读卡器的情况，或者想要在手机或平板电脑上轻松预览照片，则它可能会派上用场。
这些东芝网卡更有趣的是，它们本质上是被编程为充当无线接入点(WAP)的。您可以设置自定义SSID和密码-直接连接到2.4 GHz以上的卡。
看了看文件系统后，我意识到卡上托管的主要相册应用程序本质上是一个静态网站-作为基本的HTML、CSS和JavaScript。
我对这项技术感到兴奋的是，有可能构建一个完全独立的微服务器，能够托管几个页面和服务。
我本科四年级的建筑工作室名为“暗室”，由莫娜·马霍尔教授。工作室被分成三个展览：金字塔展览、服务器展览和后台展览。这些展览旨在探索可见性和不可见性之间的空间。
在第二个展览“服务器”中，我们被要求考虑设计一个反人类的空间-一个黑暗、寒冷、专为机器建造的电子档案。在我的初步研究中，我对亚当·巴索尔这样的艺术家特别感兴趣。
他的项目“USB&34；Dead Drop”从间谍使用的信息共享策略中获得了灵感。预先确定的秘密位置-例如掏空的岩石、砖块、原木或其他物体-将被用来谨慎地藏匿重要物品。
一旦物品--可能是一张纸条或一把钥匙--掉到地上，第二方就可以取回它，而不会直接与另一个人互动，也不会在交换过程中被发现。
虽然USB已经是一种更隐形的存储和交换信息的方式，但我想把这个概念更进一步。使用FlashAir卡，我提出了一种新的无线死线，它可能和USB一样实惠和简单，但具有无数优越的质量。
直接写入该卡需要SD卡插槽；但是，无线功能无需台式机或笔记本电脑即可供电。典型的SD卡在大约30 mA时需要2.4-3.6V电压。我发现，通过使用SD转USB适配器，可以从标准的5V USB插座为无线模块供电。
根据死点位置的不同，可以将其隐藏在一个错误的接线盒中，该接线盒将插入标准的2插座120V交流电顶部。如果-按照最初的USB Dead Drop的精神-你想把卡嵌入砖墙，那么设备可以由USB电源组供电。
我将该项目命名为warTor&#34；无线匿名repo&#34；+TOR。虽然它没有以任何方式连接到Tor网络，我认为这个标题抓住了匿名的精神(加上我想使用一些精灵精灵)。如果有人想试一下，东芝网卡可以设置为无线网桥…。从托尔之门？
我想尝试的另一件事是通过小型太阳能电池为卡供电，但我还没有做到这一点。无线死机的最大好处是，人们可以用手机登录WAP，上传或下载文件，没有人能察觉到正在发生任何形式的交换。不过，我也在开发一个可以在卡片上托管的客户端聊天应用程序。
实现后，两个人可以坐在咖啡馆里通过专用网络来回发送消息；或者，某人可以登录到网络并键入一条消息，供另一个人稍后查看。在这种情况下，两个人可以坐在咖啡馆里，通过私人网络来回发送消息；或者，某人可以登录到网络并键入消息供另一个人稍后查看。
以下是应用程序👆的一些屏幕截图。我将通过GitHub链接到我的卡上正在使用的FlashAir配置文件进行后续发布。我还将上传原始warTOR服务器的源代码，其中包含如何在野外部署您自己的warTOR的说明！
希望你喜欢这个项目。如果你愿意，请订阅更新(实况流，新帖子)，或者在Twitter上关注我，或者在下面留言！感谢收看“松懈”节目。
note_en:
Oddly, I don't remember when or how I got my hands on the  Toshiba  FlashAir card. These WiFi-enabled SD cards are made to transfer photos from a digital camera to a computer.
       Imaginging that someone would use a 4MBps wireless connection to transfer photos when the hardware transfer is closer to 70MBps seems ridiculous.
  However, it could come in handy if you're in a situation where you don't have access to an SD card reader, or want to easily preview photos on a phone or tablet.
    What's far more interesting about these Toshiba cards is the fact that they are essentially programmed to act as a  wireless access point (WAP). You can setup a custom SSID and password— connecting directly to the card over 2.4GHz.
  After peeking at the filesystem, I realized that the main photo gallery application hosted on the card is essentially a static website— served as basic HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
       My excitement with this bit of tech was the possibility of building a totally discrete microserver that would be capable of hosting a few pages and services.
    My fourth-year undergraduate architecture studio, entitled  Dark Rooms, was taught by  Mona Mahall. The studio was split into three exhibitions:  Pyramid, Server and Backstage. These exhibitions were meant to explore the "spaces between visibility and invisibility".
  In the second exhibition,  Server, we were asked to consider the design of an anti-human space— a dark, cold, electronic archive built exclusively for machines. In my preliminary research, I was particularly interested in artists like  Adam Bartholl.
  His project, a USB " dead drop", drew inspiration from an information-sharing tactic used by spies. A predetermined secret location— such as a hollowed-out rock, brick, log or other object— would be used to discreetly stash an important item.
       Once the item— perhaps a slip of paper or a key— is "dropped", a second party could then retrieve it without interacting directly with the other person or being detected in the exchange.
    While a USB is already a much more "invisible" way of storing and exchanging information, I wanted to take this concept a step further. Using the  FlashAir cards, I proposed a new wireless dead drop that could be just as affordable and simple as the USB, but with a myriad of superior qualities.
  Writing directly to the card requires an SD card slot; however, the wireless functionality can be powered without a desktop or laptop. A typical SD card takes 2.4-3.6V at about 30mA. I found that by using an SD-to-USB adapter, one could power the wireless module from a standard 5V USB outlet.
       Depending on the location of the dead drop, this could be concealed within a false junction box that would be plugged in over the top of a standard 2-outlet 120V AC. If— in the spirit of the original USB dead drop— you wanted to embed the card in a brick wall, then the device could be powered from a USB powerbank.
       I named the project  warTOR for "wireless anonymous repo" +  TOR. Though it's not connected to the Tor network in any way, I thought this title captured the spirit of anonymity (plus I wanted to use some Pokémon sprites). If someone would like to try it, the Toshiba cards  can be setup as a wireless bridge... from a Tor gateway?
    Something else that I'd like to try is powering the card via a small solar cell, but I haven't gotten there yet... The greatest benefit of the wireless dead drop is that one could log into the WAP from their cellphone, upload or download a file, and no one could discern that any sort of exchange was happening. However, I'm also working on a clientside chat application that could be hosted on the cards.
  With this implemented, then two people could sit down in a café and send messages back and forth over the private network; or, someone could login to the network and type out a message for the other person to see at a later date.
       Here are some screenshots from the application 👆. I'll be doing a follow-up post with a GitHub link to the  FlashAir CONFIG file that I'm using on my card. I'll also upload the source code for the original  warTOR server with instructions on how you can deploy your own  warTOR in the wild!
  Hope you enjoyed this project. If you'd like, please subscribe for updates (livestreams, new posts) or follow me on Twitter or leave a note below! Thanks for reading  loosed.
title: 支持WiFi的SD卡上的聊天服务器
title_en: Chat server on a WiFi-enabled SD card

“戈登还活着？！”屠夫、面包师、烛台制造者、女王、首相--所有存在的人都想让我说这句话。这是一个很棒的庆祝感叹词。这只会让每个人都开心。这一定是电影史上最著名的台词之一。
我在约克郡的戈德索普长大。每周六，我们都会去帝国影城和巴斯特·克拉布一起看“闪电侠戈登”。他是个很棒很敏感的闪电侠戈登。它有令人惊叹的音乐和特效，有龙、狮子人、行走的炸弹和居住在火星上、说话倒退的泥人。你每周会有一集半小时的节目。闪电侠最后总是看起来快要死了，让你想知道他将如何逃脱。我们走出电影院，跑下废弃的铁路路堤，玩起了胆大妄为的游戏。我会假装是伏尔坦跳过灌木丛。我做梦也没想到有一天我会把他演成一个40多岁的成年人。我被邀请到埃尔斯特里去见制片人迪诺·德·劳伦蒂斯和导演迈克·霍奇斯。墙上都是我的画像。我想：“他们在骗我--他们已经让我出演了。”所以我说：“那就是我。”他们说：“没有。”我正好是漫画里那个角色的模样。迪诺说：“我们想不出其他人来出演这个角色。”我说：“如果你把它给别人，我就掐死那个混蛋。”
我对那些说这是一部夏令营电影的人很挑剔。不是这样的。太完美了。肯尼斯·布拉纳、帕特里克·斯图尔特和德里克·雅各比都同意我的观点：这是一部杰作。这是太空第一次不只是一片黑色的天空。女王的音乐是令人惊叹的，就像当Flash和Dale乘坐火箭上升，随着太空舱中的女王的声音(爱情主题)崩溃在彼此的怀里。
布景也很棒。每个人都有完美的演员阵容。这是一部集色彩、设计和表演于一身的杰作，具有绝妙的连环画风格。他们绝不能，永远不能考虑重新做这件事。总有一些笨蛋想要重复一些事情，但他们不会比现在更好，所以他们最好不要碰他们那该死的手。
因为他们将建造如此庞大的布景，迪诺·德·劳伦蒂斯一开始就想要计划一部续集，于是我被邀请执导闪电侠戈登2。“我不是这方面的导演，”我说。“我对特效、闪光戈登或其他任何美国漫画一无所知。”我是在“丹迪与比诺”中长大的。我们就到此为止了，但后来迪诺和第一位导演闹翻了，又来找我执导第一部电影。他带着一本“闪电侠戈登”(Flash Gordon)的丰厚趣味书，用协和式飞机把我送到了纽约。我遇到了服装设计师丹尼洛·多纳蒂(Danilo Donati)，带着恐惧和恐惧，我说：“好的。我会做的。“。
我们对天空有一个问题，直到特效小组想出了一种方法，通过向盐水中注入颜色并放慢镜头的速度来形成云层。我们的演员阵容很大程度上是为了与20世纪30年代的连环漫画中的外观相匹配。表演紧随其后。闪电侠必须有一个轮廓分明的下巴，这个全美男孩。迪诺的岳母在电视节目“好莱坞广场”中看到山姆·J·琼斯，所以我们用飞机送他过去。我知道他有烂番茄的评论，但闪电侠不应该是抽屉里最锋利的刀子。他真的是无辜的，你可以相信他是一名足球运动员。
戴尔一定是那个活泼的纽约女孩。我不知道是谁决定鹰人应该留胡子的。我记得看着威尔士在橄榄球比赛中把英格兰打得落花流水，电话响了，是迪诺说的：“穿红衣的球队。九号。五号。四号。第一位。鹰人。“。我说：“在我们拍摄他们的时候，他们不会想要吊在电线上六个月的。”他挂断了电话，威尔士橄榄球队从此再也没有被提起过。布莱恩·布莱德在电影开始时出现，但一直消失到接近尾声。他以如此巨大的精力扮演了这个角色，我非常感激。
这部电影在两个层面上起作用。这是一部适合儿童观看的周六早上的动作片，但在另一个层面上，我认为它对一些男人的性幻想产生了严重的影响。他们喜欢性感的奥拉公主，40年后，小时候看过的8岁孩子现在可以和自己的孩子一起看了。孩子们会看到无辜的一面，成年人会看到性的一面，这个圈子会重新开始。
note_en:
“GORDON’S ALIVE?!” The butcher, the baker, the candlestick-maker, queens, prime ministers – everybody who exists wants me to say that line. It’s a great celebratory exclamation. It just makes everyone happy. It’s got to be one of the most famous lines in film history.
 I grew up in Goldthorpe, Yorkshire. Every Saturday, we’d go to the Empire to watch  Flash Gordon with Buster Crabbe. He was a wonderful, sensitive Flash Gordon. It had marvellous music and special effects with dragons, lion men, walking bombs and the Clay People who lived on Mars and spoke backwards. You’d get one half-hour episode each week. Flash would always end up looking like he was about to die, leaving you wondering how he would escape. We’d come out of the cinema and run down the disused railway embankment to play dares. I’d jump over the bushes pretending to be Vultan. I never dreamed that one day I’d play him as a grown man in my 40s.  I was invited to Elstree to meet the producer Dino De Laurentiis and the director Mike Hodges. There were all these paintings of me on the walls. I thought: “The sods are having me on – they’ve already cast me.” So I said: “That’s me.” They said: “No.” I just happened to be the spitting image of the character from the comic. Dino said: “We can’t think of anyone else for the part.” I said: “If you offer it to anyone else, I’ll strangle the bastard.”
  I’m very critical of people who say it’s a camp film. It’s not. It’s perfection. Kenneth Branagh, Patrick Stewart and Derek Jacobi all agree with me: it’s a masterpiece. It was the first time space wasn’t just a black sky. And Queen’s music is amazing, like when Flash and Dale go up in the rocket and collapse into each other’s arms to the sound of Queen’s In The Space Capsule (The Love Theme).
 The sets are marvellous, too. Everyone’s perfectly cast. It’s a masterpiece of colour, design and acting, with a wonderful comic-strip style. They must never, ever entertain redoing it. There’s always some tosser who wants to repeat things, but they won’t ever better it, so they’d better keep their bloody hands off.
  Because they were going to be building such massive sets, Dino De Laurentiis wanted to plan a sequel right at the outset, and I was offered the job of directing Flash Gordon 2. “I am the wrong director for this,” I said. “I don’t know about special effects or Flash Gordon or any other American comics.” I was brought up on the Dandy and  the Beano. We left it at that, but then Dino fell out with the first director and came after me again to direct the first film. He flew me out to New York on Concorde with a bumper fun book of Flash Gordon. I met Danilo Donati, the costume designer, and with fear and trepidation, I said: “OK. I’ll do it.”
  We had a problem with the skies, until the special-effects team came up with a way of forming clouds by injecting colours into saline water and slowing the footage down. We largely cast to match the looks from the 1930s strip cartoon. The acting just followed. Flash had to have a chiselled jaw, the all-American boy. Dino’s mother-in-law saw Sam J Jones on the TV show Hollywood Squares, so we flew him over. I know he’s got Rotten Tomato reviews, but Flash isn’t supposed to be the sharpest knife in the drawer. He’s genuinely innocent and you can believe he’s a football player.
 Dale had to be the perky, New York girl. I don’t know who decided that the Hawkmen should have beards. I remember watching Wales beating the hell out of England in the rugby and the phone rang and it was Dino who said: “The team in red. Number nine. Number five. Number four. Number one. Hawkmen.” I said: “They’re not going to want to hang on wires for six months while we film them.” He hung up and the Welsh rugby team were never mentioned again.  Brian Blessed comes at the beginning of the film but disappears until nearly the end. He played the role with such enormous energy that I was extremely grateful.
 The film works on two levels. It’s a Saturday morning action movie for children but, on another level, I think it had a serious effect on some men’s sexual fantasies. They loved the voluptuous Princess Aura.Forty years later, the eight-year-olds who watched it as children can now watch it with their own children. The children will see the innocent side and the adults will see the sexual side and the circle will start again.
title: 我们如何制作闪电侠戈登-布莱恩·布莱斯和迈克·霍奇斯著
title_en: How We Made Flash Gordon – By Brian Blessed and Mike Hodges

note:
几十年来，作为一名好莱坞高管，最好的事情真的是你是如何被解雇的。制片厂的高管会逐渐地、温和地，甚至是充满爱意地被推到一边，给他们几个月的时间来塑造他们自己的叙述，找到新的工作，甚至提拔。2015年，当艾米·帕斯卡(Amy Pascal)被索尼影业(Sony Pictures)赶出时，她得到了一份退出方案和一份据报道价值4000万美元的制作协议。
当然，那是在流媒体服务出现之前，以无情的逻辑和冷酷的效率颠覆了一切。
8月7日，当华纳传媒(WarnerMedia)突然裁掉数百名员工的工作岗位，清空了这家曾经建造好莱坞、现在是AT&amp；T子公司的伟大制片厂的高管套房时，这一点再明显不过了。在一系列欢快的视频通话中，那些自认为是制片厂显赫人物的高管们被提醒，他们在--或曾经--在一家电话公司的视频部门工作。在一系列欢快的视频通话中，人们提醒那些自认为是制片厂显赫人物的高管，他们在--或曾经--在一家电话公司的视频部门工作。与他交谈的两个人告诉我，华纳媒体娱乐公司的董事长鲍勃·格林布拉特在新闻发布的当天早上得知自己被解雇了。杰弗里·施莱辛格(Jeffrey Schlesinger)是一位在公司工作了37年的资深人士，经营着利润丰厚的国际许可业务。另外两个人告诉我，他向朋友抱怨说，他提前不到一小时就收到了通知。
前好莱坞记者联席总裁贾尼斯·敏(Janice Min)曾短暂担任过流媒体平台Quibi的高管，她说，“正如这里的人所知，我们正处于好莱坞残酷的最后一幕，因为流媒体投资和基础设施占据了主导地位。”“对最高层的礼貌和生产交易，更重要的是，让官僚机构浮出水面的金融消防水龙似乎正在消失。”这就像一个俱乐部，已经因为大流行而关闭，没有会费来养活所有的会员。“。
华纳的这部电视剧标志着一个转折点，部分原因是它的巨大规模及其旗下标志性公司的高调：华纳兄弟(Warner Brothers)、HBO和CNN。而与此同时，好莱坞的力量显然没有出现在国家对话中。华盛顿被TikTok所消费，TikTok是中国人拥有的视频分享应用，是世界上最成功的新内容平台。TikTok已经成功地成为Quibi-好莱坞用户生成内容的高端替代品-努力寻找观众。这位刚刚被提名为副总统候选人的加州政客来自旧金山，她并没有特别宣传自己与好莱坞的关系(尽管上周她在好莱坞内部人士的Instagram上到处都是)。
最近几天，WarnerMedia和NBC Universal的公司转变表明，你多年来一直在读到的技术转变终于以具体的形式出现了，大流行加速了这一转变。新的行业领袖想要谈论数字产品和订阅营销。娱乐业高管最有趣的简介是，字面意义上的讣告，特别是维亚康姆创始人萨姆纳·雷石东职业生涯中的成功和恶习目录。
(就像他所在行业的许多人一样，上周去世的雷石东先生享年97岁，他坚持的时间比任何人预期的都要长得多。维亚康姆的前员工回忆说，六年多前，时任首席执行官菲利普·道曼(Philippe Dauman)要求他的助手为当时90岁的雷石东起草一篇激动人心的悼词，并创建一个纪念他的网站。但多曼四年前就被解雇了，目前还没有让他发表悼词的计划，该网站仍在某个被遗忘的数字货架上。)。
好莱坞现在发生的很多事情也让人期待已久的死亡感觉，以至于你几乎认为自己刚刚错过了葬礼。在华纳传媒(WarnerMedia)，在推出一项流媒体服务-HBO Go、HBO Now或HBO Max-没有人知道它的名字后，高管们被解雇了。到目前为止，这项服务的突出之处主要是它积极而失败的尝试，试图将大约400万实际使用这项服务的人增加到3000万人以上。
另一位突然被迫离职的高管凯文·赖利(Kevin Reilly)告诉“好莱坞报道”：“这是一个伟大的清算。”
这一清算主要是由平淡无奇的流媒体经济推动的，尽管它也与今年更知名的种族和性别清算有重叠之处。制片厂的高管们一直被“关于我们”的页面所羞辱-这些页面上都是他们领导人的个人资料-随着争取代表权的努力增加了要求变革的新压力，这些页面都是白人面孔。
但潜在的理由是经济的，也是显而易见的。“有线电视的金色财源正在枯竭。由于大多数公司唯一的增长业务来自流媒体，而流媒体还没有盈利，这些公司别无选择，只能削减成本。“The Information”写道。(华纳计划裁员的消息泄露给了那家总部位于硅谷的商业刊物，而不是通常的好莱坞交易，雪上加霜。)。
该行业的新领军者并不是来自旧好莱坞，旧好莱坞已经见证了它的俱乐部性质和价值观声名狼藉。新任华纳传媒(WarnerMedia)首席执行官杰森·基拉尔(Jason Kilar)在职业生涯的形成阶段担任亚马逊(Amazon)全球应用软件高级副总裁，亚马逊以其冷酷的企业文化而闻名。他经营着Hulu，然后在与其传统媒体所有者发生冲突后离开了Hulu。在华纳传媒，他提拔了一位在好莱坞俱乐部没有取得职业生涯的高管安·萨诺夫(Ann Sarnoff)领导他的内容部门。
许多新领导人都是Netflix文化的崇拜者，这种文化既务实又不带感情：高管们在自助餐厅用餐，在一张令人钦佩的幻灯片演示中，他们的公司哲学认为，员工就像运动员。经理们应该总是寻求升级，如果有更好的球员出现，即使是表现优异的人也会被解雇。(与公司首席执行官里德·黑斯廷斯(Reed Hastings)一起设计演示文稿的备受尊敬的人力资源高管最终被解雇。)。
WarnerMedia的基拉尔在一封电子邮件中告诉我，他的裁员和重组旨在推动公司“从批发思维模式转变为零售思维模式”-也就是从旧的制片厂热门制作人与发行商握手交易，到技术人员专注于用户友好的流媒体界面和订户留住。
哥伦比亚广播公司电影公司(CBS Films)前总裁特里·普莱斯(Terry Press)说，“这就是进入电影业的人和从事内容业务的人之间的区别。”今年早些时候，该部门在与维亚康姆(Viacom)的合并中被取消。
该行业的文化转变也正在抹去领地。在WarnerMedia解雇的前一天，NBC环球(NBC Universal)迫使四面楚歌的娱乐部门董事长下台-这一传奇职位过去由格林布拉特等人担任，并宣布不会取代他。相反，它正在剥离高管角色，将大部分曾经是独立运营的业务合并到Syfy和NBC等不同的渠道。同样，WarnerMedia将其皇冠上的明珠HBO、日常有线电视频道TBS和TNT以及苦苦挣扎的新流媒体服务合并在一起。
这些公司否认组织变革会影响你所看到的。(NBC的一位官员说，“这些品牌将保持其独特性，观众看不到明显的差异。”)。但在衰退的行业中，情况通常不是这样。这些举措呼应了长期衰落的出版业机构的做法，比如康泰纳仕(CondéNast)杂志公司，该公司逐渐将“Vogue”和“名利场”(Vanity Fair)等杂志的高管角色合并在一起，同时坚称他们不是在削弱不可避免的衰落品牌。
而在WarnerMedia，挑战尤其事关生死存亡。本月的裁员是否像基拉尔和AT&amp；T；的首席执行官约翰·斯坦基(John Stankey)想要的那样，预示着一次成功的转变，还是仅仅是为了掩盖该公司在流媒体领域的显著失败，在几年内我们都不会知道。HBO Max几乎无法与Netflix和迪士尼竞争，尽管它的服务充满了人们喜爱的节目和电影，从最受欢迎的阿尔弗雷德·希区柯克(Alfred Hitchcock)到HBO的长期热门节目，包括今年夏天上映的“洛夫克拉夫特乡村”(Lovcraft Country)和“我可能会毁了你”(I May Destroy You)。
随着顶级创意高管的清洗完成，HBO Max和有线电视频道内部内容的责任将主要落在凯西·布洛伊斯(Casey Bloys)身上，他是HBO的资深人士，目前负责华纳媒体的所有娱乐内容。他在一次电话采访中说，他已经告诉他的新团队，他希望在流媒体服务上制作节目，以补充HBO最出名的“守望者”(Watchman)和“继承者”(Success)等热播、复杂的成人节目。他指出，像“绿灯侠”和“绯闻女孩”这样吸引年轻观众的简单有趣的标题是扩大服务范围的典范。他的成功在一定程度上将取决于该公司明确营销其流媒体服务的能力，或许更多的取决于AT&amp；T是否真的愿意继续在Netflix和迪士尼等电视节目上投入资金。
布洛伊斯先生是一位伟大的程序员，而不是旧模式的权势玩家或政治家。事实上，制片厂老板似乎已经失去了他们在美国权力结构中的中心位置，变成了普通公司的薪酬丰厚的员工，普通的人关注的是底线。有一个例外，迪士尼也证明了这一规则：鲍勃·伊格尔(Bob Iger)的迪士尼+正好赶上了流媒体浪潮，提供了一项满足冠状病毒时刻的业务。
巴里·迪勒(Barry Diller)曾是派拉蒙和福克斯的负责人，现在是数字媒体公司IAC的首席执行官，他说，“迪士尼在未来仍将是重要的。”“其他人都是高尔夫球场的球童，他们永远都不会打。”
note_en:
For decades, the best thing about being a Hollywood executive, really, was how you got fired. Studio executives would be gradually, gently, even lovingly, nudged aside, given months to shape their own narratives and find new work, or even promoted. When Amy Pascal was pushed out of Sony Pictures in 2015, she got an exit package and production deal  worth a reported $40 million.
 That, of course, was before streaming services arrived, upending everything with a ruthless logic and coldhearted efficiency.
 That was never more clear than on Aug. 7, when WarnerMedia abruptly eliminated the jobs of hundreds of employees, emptying the executive suite at the once-great studio that built Hollywood, and is now the subsidiary of AT&T. In a series of brisk video calls, executives who imagined they were studio eminences were reminded that they work — or used to work — at the video division of a phone company. The chairman of WarnerMedia Entertainment, Bob Greenblatt, learned that he’d been fired the morning of the day the news broke, two people he spoke to told me. Jeffrey Schlesinger, a 37-year company veteran who ran the lucrative international licensing business, complained to friends that he had less than an hour’s notice, two other people told me.
 “We’re in the brutal final scenes of Hollywood as people here knew it, as streaming investment and infrastructure take precedence,” said Janice Min, the former Hollywood Reporter co-president who did a brief stretch as an executive at the streaming platform Quibi. “Politesse and production deal kiss-offs for those at the top, and, more importantly, the financial fire hose to float a bureaucracy, seem to be disappearing. It’s like a club, already shut down by the pandemic, running out of dues to feed all its members.”
 The drama at Warner marked a turning point, in part because of its huge size and the high profile of the iconic companies under its umbrella: Warner Brothers, HBO and CNN among them. And it comes as Hollywood power is conspicuously absent from the national conversation.  Washington is consumed by TikTok, the Chinese-owned video-sharing app that’s the most successful new content platform in the world. TikTok has succeeded as Quibi — Hollywood’s premium alternative to user-generated content — struggles to find an audience. The California politician just nominated for the vice presidency comes from San Francisco, and doesn’t particularly advertise her Hollywood ties (though she was all over Hollywood insiders’ Instagram last week).
 The corporate shifts at WarnerMedia and NBCUniversal in recent days signal that the technological shift you’ve been reading about for years is finally taking concrete form, accelerated by the pandemic. The new leaders of the industry want to talk about digital products and subscription marketing. The most interesting profiles of entertainment executives are, literally, obituaries, notably the catalog of victories and vices that marked the career of Viacom’s founder, Sumner Redstone.
  (Like much of his industry,  Mr. Redstone, who died last week at age 97, held on far longer than anyone expected. Former Viacom employees recalled that it had been more than six years since, the then- chief executive, Philippe Dauman, asked his aides to draft a stirring eulogy for Mr. Redstone, who was 90 at the time, and to create a website in his memory. But Mr. Dauman was fired four years ago, there are no plans for him to deliver a eulogy and the website remains on some forgotten digital shelf.)
 Much of what’s happening now in Hollywood, too, has that feeling of a death so long anticipated that you half assumed you’d just missed the funeral. At WarnerMedia, the executives’ firings came after the company badly botched the introduction of a streaming service whose name — HBO Go, HBO Now, or HBO Max — nobody could figure out. The service has primarily distinguished itself so far by its energetic and unsuccessful attempts to spin about 4 million people who have actually used the service into a number north of 30 million.
 “It’s the great reckoning,” another top executive who was abruptly forced out, Kevin Reilly, told  The Hollywood Reporter.
  That reckoning is mostly driven by the unglamorous economics of streaming, though it also overlaps with this year’s better-known reckoning, over race and gender. Studio executives have been mortified by the “About Us” pages with profiles of their leaders — pages that are full of white faces as the push for representation adds new pressure for change.
 But the underlying rationale is economic, and obvious. “The golden rivers of money from cable TV are drying up. With the only growth business for most of the companies coming from streaming, which isn’t a profit maker yet, the companies have no alternative than to cut costs,”  The Information wrote. (News of Warner’s planned layoffs leaked to that Silicon Valley-based business publication, not the usual Hollywood trades, adding insult to injury.)
 The new leaders in the industry do not come out of old Hollywood, which has seen its clubbiness and values fall into disrepute. The new WarnerMedia chief executive, Jason Kilar, spent the formative years of his career as the senior vice president of worldwide application software at Amazon, known for its  grim corporate culture. He ran Hulu, then left it after clashing with its legacy media owners. At WarnerMedia he promoted an executive who hadn’t made her career inside the Hollywood club, Ann Sarnoff, to head his content division.
 Many of the new leaders are admirers of the culture at Netflix, which is hardheaded and unsentimental: Executives eat in the cafeteria and have a corporate philosophy that holds, in an admired  slide presentation, that employees are like athletes. Managers should always be looking to trade up, and fire even high performers if a better player comes along. (The well-regarded human resources executive who developed the presentation with the company’s chief executive, Reed Hastings, was, herself, eventually  fired.)
 WarnerMedia’s Mr. Kilar told me in an email that his cuts and reorganizations were aimed at pushing the company “from a wholesaling mind set to a retailing mind set” — that is, from the old studio hitmakers’ handshake deals with distributors to a techie’s focus on user-friendly streaming interfaces and subscriber retention.
   “This is the difference between people who got into the movie business and people who are in the content business,” said Terry Press, the former president of CBS Films, whose division was eliminated in a merger with Viacom earlier this year.
 The industry’s cultural shift is also wiping out fiefdoms. A day before the WarnerMedia firings, NBCUniversal forced out the embattled chairman of its entertainment division — a storied role held in the past by, among others, Mr. Greenblatt — and announced it wouldn’t replace him. Instead it’s shearing off executive roles and merging most of what were once separate operations across channels as varied as Syfy and NBC. Similarly, WarnerMedia combined its crown jewel, HBO, and the workaday cable channels TBS and TNT and the struggling new streaming service.
 The companies deny that the organizational changes will affect what you see. (“The brands will maintain their distinctiveness, and there won’t be visible differences to the viewer,” an NBC official said.) But that’s not how it usually plays out in declining industries. The moves echo those taken by long-declining publishing industry institutions like the magazine company Condé Nast, which has gradually combined the roles of executives at magazines like Vogue and Vanity Fair, all the while insisting that they weren’t diminishing the inevitably diminished brands.
 And at WarnerMedia, the challenge is particularly existential. We won’t know for a couple of years whether this month’s layoffs signaled a successful shift, as Mr. Kilar and AT&T’s chief executive, John Stankey, intend, or whether they were simply a clumsy attempt to mask the company’s remarkable failure in the streaming world. HBO Max has barely been able to compete with Netflix and Disney, despite having a service full of beloved shows and movies, from the best of Alfred Hitchcock to HBO’s long hot streak that includes, this summer, the releases of “Lovecraft Country” and “I May Destroy You."
  With the purge of top creative executives completed, the responsibility for what’s inside HBO Max and the cable TV channels will fall largely on Casey Bloys, an HBO veteran who is now overseeing all of WarnerMedia’s entertainment content. He has, he said in a telephone interview, told his new team that he wants programming on the streaming service that will complement the buzzy, complex adult shows like “Watchmen” and “Succession” that HBO is best known for. He is pointed to straightforwardly fun titles that appeal to younger audiences like “Green Lantern” and “Gossip Girl" as models for broadening out the service. His success will depend, in part, on the company’s ability to clearly market its streaming service and perhaps more on whether AT&T is really willing to keep spending on TV like Netflix and Disney.
 Mr. Bloys is a great programmer, not a power player or politician of the old model. Indeed, the studio bosses seem to have lost their central place in the American power structure and become simply the well-compensated employees of ordinary companies, with ordinary attention to the bottom line. There is one exception, Disney, which also proves the rule: Bob Iger’s Disney+ started just in time to catch the streaming wave and provide a business that met the coronavirus moment.
 “Disney will remain relevant into the future,” said Barry Diller, who once headed Paramount and Fox and is now chief executive of the digital media company IAC. “All of the rest of them are caddies on a golf course they’ll never play.”
title: 老好莱坞终于去世的那一周
title_en: The Week Old Hollywood Finally, Died

大约一年前，SUSE还向基金会贡献了KubeCF项目，该基金会本身允许Cloud Foundry应用运行时-Cloud Foundry的核心-在Kubernetes之上运行。
“在SUSE，我们尽可能先在上游发展，”SUSE工程与创新总裁托马斯·迪·贾科莫(Thomas Di Giacomo)说。因此，在今年早些时候体验了为基金会贡献KubeCF的价值后，我们决定再次这样做，对Cloud Foundry社区和EiriniX团队都是有利的。自从KubeCF成为一个基金会项目以来，我们看到它的贡献和使用量都在上升，这表明越来越多的组织正在将开发人员的时间投入到上游。将EiriniX贡献给基金会是让更广泛社区参与的必经之路。“。
SuSE在一年前首次展示了EiriniX。例如，该工具实现了一些功能，比如能够通过SSH进入容器并对其进行调试，或者使用KubeCF的替代日志记录解决方案。
Cloud Foundry Foundation的执行董事Chip Childers说：“向基金会贡献这个项目具有重大价值，因为它确保了寻找类似解决方案的其他项目团队不会在Eirini周围创建扩展项目，而不会重新发明轮子，”Cloud Foundry Foundation的执行董事奇普·奇尔德斯(Chip Childers)说。“既然EiriniX已经存在于基金会内部，开发者就可以充分利用其对Eirini的加载件库，修改Cloud Foundry的核心功能。我很高兴看到这个项目所有尚未发明的用例。“
note_en:
About a year ago,  SUSE   also  contributed the KubeCF project to the foundation, which itself allows the  Cloud Foundry Application Runtime — the core of Cloud Foundry — to run on top of Kubernetes.
   “At SUSE we are developing upstream first as much as possible,” said Thomas Di Giacomo, president of Engineering and Innovation at SUSE. “So, after experiencing the value of contributing  KubeCF to the Foundation earlier this year, we decided it would be beneficial to both the Cloud Foundry community and the  EiriniX team to do it again. We have seen an uptick in contributions to and usage of KubeCF since it became a Foundation project, indicating that more organizations are investing developer time into the upstream. Contributing  EiriniX to the Foundation is a surefire way to get the broader community involved.”
 SUSE first demonstrated EiriniX a year ago. The tool implements features like the ability to SSH into a container and debug it, for example, or to use alternative logging solutions for KubeCF.
 “There is significant value in contributing this project to the Foundation, as it ensures that other project teams looking for a similar solution to creating Extensions around Eirini will not reinvent the wheel,” said  Chip Childers,   executive director, Cloud Foundry Foundation. “Now that  EiriniX exists within the Foundation, developers can take full advantage of its library of add-ons to Eirini and modify core features of Cloud Foundry. I’m excited to see all of the use cases for this project that have not yet been invented.”
title: SuSE为Cloud Foundry Foundation贡献EiriniX
title_en: SUSE contributes EiriniX to the Cloud Foundry Foundation

Canalys没有提供具体数字，但它确实设定了零售领域的整体市场地位，微软位居第二，亚马逊位居第三，阿里巴巴和IBM紧随其后，分别位居第四和第五。
谷歌对零售业的追逐可能不是巧合。许多零售商不想将他们的云业务放在AWS上，因为亚马逊与这些零售商直接竞争。CRM Essentials的创始人兼首席分析师布伦特·利里(Brent Leary)表示，正因为如此，这个消息并不真的让他感到惊讶。
Leary告诉TechCrunch：“零售商必须与亚马逊竞争，我猜他们最不想做的就是使用AWS，帮助亚马逊资助所有在某些情况下会被用来对抗他们的新举措和实验。”此外，他还表示，许多零售商也更愿意将他们的客户数据排除在亚马逊的服务之外。
Canalys高级总监亚历克斯·史密斯(Alex Smith)表示，亚马逊效应与大流行和其他技术因素相结合，一直对谷歌有利，至少在零售领域是这样。“现在，零售商比以往任何时候都更需要一种数字战略，才能在全方位渠道的世界中取胜，特别是在亚马逊占据在线主导地位的情况下。从客户体验到成本优化，数字应用无处不在，零售商的整体技术能力将决定其成功与否。“。
新冠肺炎曾迫使许多零售商长时间关闭门店，当你把这一点与人们在开门时更不愿进入门店结合起来时，如果零售商还没有大量的在线业务，他们就不得不参加电子商务速成课程。
Canalys指出，谷歌已经用其广告和搜索能力吸引了客户，而不仅仅是纯粹的基础设施产品，利用其其他优势来扩大细分市场。
认识到这一点，谷歌一直在大力推动零售业务，包括与Salesforce建立大规模合作伙伴关系，以及去年在Google Cloud上发布的特定产品。正如我们在零售发售时所写的那样，
该公司提供专门为在线零售商设计的电子商务主机，并提供一项特殊的高级计划，这样零售商就可以获得“技术架构审查和旺季运营支持…的白手套待遇”。据该公司称。换句话说，它希望帮助这些公司在网站因需求而关闭时避免灾难性的、亏损的结果。
更重要的是，Canalys报告称，谷歌云也一直在积极招聘，并与大型系统集成商建立合作伙伴关系，以帮助增长零售业务。零售客户包括家得宝、科尔百货、好市多和百思买。
note_en:
Canalys   didn’t provide specific numbers, but it did set overall market positions in the retail sector with Microsoft coming in second, Amazon third, followed by Alibaba and IBM in fourth and fifth respectively.
   It’s probably not a coincidence that Google went after retail. Many retailers don’t want to put their cloud presence onto AWS, as Amazon.com competes directly with these retailers. Brent Leary, founder and principal analyst at CRM Essentials, says that as such, the news doesn’t really surprise him.
 “Retailers have to compete with Amazon, and I’m guessing the last thing they want to do is use AWS and help Amazon fund all their new initiatives and experiments that in some cases will be used against them,” Leary told TechCrunch. Further, he said that many retailers would also prefer to keep their customer data off of Amazon’s services.
 Canalys Senior Director Alex Smith says that this Amazon effect combined with the pandemic and other technological factors has been working in Google’s favor, at least in the retail sector. “Now more than ever, retailers need a digital strategy to win in an omnichannel world, especially with Amazon’s online dominance. Digital is applied everywhere from customer experience to cost optimization, and the overall technological capability of a retailer is what will define its success,” he said.
 COVID-19 has forced many retailers to close stores for extended periods of time, and when you combine that with people being more reluctant to go inside stores when they do open, retailers have had to take a crash course in eCommerce if they didn’t have a significant online presence already.
 Canalys points out that Google has lured customers with its advertising and search capabilities beyond just pure infrastructure offerings, taking advantage of its other strengths to grow the market segment.
 Recognizing this, Google has been making a big retail push including a big partnership with Salesforce and specific products announced at Google Cloud Next last year. As we wrote  at the time of the retail offering,
 The company offers eCommerce Hosting, designed specifically for online retailers, and it is offering a special premium program, so retailers get “white glove treatment with technical architecture reviews and peak season operations support…” according to the company. In other words, it wants to help these companies avoid disastrous, money-losing results when a site goes down due to demand.
 What’s more, Canalys reports that Google Cloud has also been hiring aggressively and forming partnerships with big systems integrators to help grow the retail business. Retail customers include Home Depot, Kohl’s, Costco and Best Buy.
title: Canalys：谷歌是在线零售商最大的云基础设施提供商
title_en: Canalys: Google is top cloud infrastructure provider for online retailers

如果你想知道，这不会只是一个很长的网络研讨会。我们有一些技术诀窍，将带来你对我们面对面活动的所有期望，从内容翔实的小组讨论到挑衅性的一对一采访，到网络交流，甚至是推介会议。虽然虚拟与我们过去的活动不同，但它提供了一个巨大的好处：使访问民主化。

获得TC Sessions：移动性的门票，聆听布莱恩·萨莱斯基(Bryan Salesky)的演讲，以及来自保时捷(Porsche)、Waymo、Lyft等公司的其他几位出色的演讲者。有限时间的门票只有145美元，团体、学生和参展的初创企业都有折扣。我们希望在那里见到你！
note_en:
Lucid is now poised to make its mark on the EV landscape. Which is why we’re excited to announce that Peter Rawlinson, the CEO and CTO of Lucid Motors will join us on our virtual stage at   TC Sessions: Mobility . The virtual event will occur over two days on October 6 and October 7, and will build off of last year’s inaugural event.
 Rawlinson has three decades of experience in the automotive industry that is now culminating with  Lucid Motors  . Prior to Lucid, Rawlinson was vice president of vehicle engineering at Tesla and chief engineer of the Model S, where he led the engineering of the Model S from a clean sheet to production readiness while building the engineering team. He was formerly head of vehicle engineering at Corus Automotive, chief engineer at Lotus Cars, and principal engineer at Jaguar Cars.
  In short, Rawlinson has institutional knowledge — and probably some good stories — about what works and what doesn’t when building a car company. While on our virtual stage, Rawlinson will discuss Lucid Motors and plans for the automaker’s future as well as the electric vehicle industry and maybe some of those interesting stories from his days at Tesla, Jaguar and Lotus Cars.
 In case you’re wondering, this won’t just be one long webinar. We have some technical tricks up our sleeves that will bring all of what you’d expect from our in-person events, from the informative panels and provocative one-on-one interviews to the networking and even a pitch-off session. While virtual isn’t the same as our events in the past, it has provided one massive benefit: democratizing access.
 If you’re a startup or investor based in Europe, Africa, Australia, South America or another region in the U.S., you can listen in, network and connect with other participants here in Silicon Valley.
 Get your tickets for  TC Sessions: Mobility to hear from Bryan Salesky, along with several other fantastic speakers from Porsche, Waymo, Lyft and more.  Tickets are just $145 for a limited time, with  discounts for groups, students and exhibiting startups. We hope to see you there!
title: Lucid Motors首席执行官兼首席技术官彼得·罗林森将加入TC Sessions：Mobility 2020
title_en: Lucid Motors CEO and CTO Peter Rawlinson to join TC Sessions: Mobility 2020

(路透社)-苹果公司(Apple Inc.)周一表示，它已经扩大了一项计划，向独立维修店提供零部件和培训，以覆盖其Mac电脑。
苹果最初在去年推出了这项计划，但只覆盖了该公司最畅销的产品iPhone。Mac用户以前要么使用苹果，要么使用授权的保修服务提供商直接从苹果获得部件修复。
苹果上个月公布季度Mac销售额增长21.6%，至70亿美元，原因是许多员工转向在家工作。根据研究公司IDC的数据，其市场份额从去年同期的6.3%增长到第二季度的7.3%。
苹果首席运营官杰夫·威廉姆斯(Jeff Williams)在一份声明中表示：“当设备需要维修时，我们希望人们能够获得安全可靠的解决方案--我们在过去一年里增加了数千个维修地点，这次最新的扩建就是其中之一。”我们期待着为我们的Mac用户带来方便和值得信赖的维修体验。“。
在苹果改变维修做法之前，维修权团体多年来一直在呼吁，批评苹果只向百思买(Best Buy Inc)等公司提供正版零部件和培训手册，这些公司将对苹果的设备进行保修相关工作。较小的商店认为，由于数量承诺，加入该计划的成本太高。
随着独立维修计划的实施，苹果开始出售零部件，并向独立商店提供免费培训课程，以执行保修期外的工作。
美国公众研究利益集团(Public Research Interest Group)维修权运动负责人内森·普罗科特(Nathan Proctor)表示，Mac项目的加入是值得欢迎的一步，但出版物“副刊”(Vivie Here)报道的独立商店协议显示了繁琐的条款。
普罗科特在一份声明中说：“归根结底，我们要么有一个自由市场来修复我们拥有的设备，要么我们没有。”
苹果公司周一表示，它已经根据独立商店和其他感兴趣的各方的反馈，更新了该项目的材料和培训。
note_en:
(Reuters) - Apple Inc said on Monday it had expanded a program that provides parts and training to independent repair shops to cover its Mac computers.
 Apple initially launched the program last year, but it only covered iPhones, the company’s biggest-selling product. Mac users previously either had to use Apple or an authorized warranty service provider to get fixes with parts directly from Apple.
 Apple last month reported an uptick of 21.6% in quarterly Mac sales to $7 billion, as many employees shifted to working from home. Its market share grew to 7.3% in the second quarter from 6.3% a year earlier, according to research firm IDC.
 “When a device needs repairs, we want people to have access to a safe and reliable solution — this latest expansion joins the thousands of repair locations we’ve added over the past year,” Jeff Williams, Apple’s chief operating officer, said in a statement. “We’re looking forward to bringing that convenient and trustworthy repair experience to our Mac users.”
 Apple’s shift in repair practices came after years of calls from right-to-repair groups who had criticized the company for, among other things, only providing genuine parts and training manuals to firms such as Best Buy Inc that were to do warranty-related work on its devices. Smaller shops argued that program was too costly to join due to volume commitments.
 With the independent repair program, Apple began offering to sell parts and provide free training courses to independent shops to perform out-of-warranty work.
 Nathan Proctor, head of the U.S. Public Research Interest Group right-to-repair campaign, said the addition of Mac's program was a "welcome" step, but the agreement for independent shops reported by publication Vice  here showed "onerous" terms.
 “At the end of the day, we either have a free market to fix the devices we own, or we don’t,” Proctor said in a statement.
 Apple on Monday said it has updated its materials and training for the program based on feedback from independent shops and other interested parties.
title: 苹果在去年仅为iPhone首次亮相后，将其独立维修店计划扩大到Mac
title_en: Apple expands its independent repair shop program to Macs, after it debuted last year for iPhones only

这种方法简明扼要，而且是伪数学的。然而，从编程的角度来看，这不是构建复杂软件的好方法。
操作张量的代码通过元组中的维标识符执行此操作。如果您想旋转您阅读评论的图像，确定需要更改的尺寸并更改它们。
定义旋转(IMS)：#Batch_Size x高度x宽度x旋转的通道=IMS。转置(1，2)#Batch_Size x宽度x高度x通道返回旋转旋转(IMS)[0]。
这段代码很简单，理论上有很好的文档记录。但是，它不反映目标函数的语义。旋转属性与批次或通道无关。在确定要更改的维度时，该函数不应考虑这些维度。
这导致了两个问题。首先，非常令人担忧的是，如果我们传入一个单例图像，则此函数运行良好，但无法正常工作。
然而，更令人担忧的是，该函数实际上可能会错误地使用BatchDimension，并将不同图像的属性混合在一起。这可能会导致令人讨厌的错误，如果对代码隐藏此维度，则很容易避免这些错误。
张量最有用的方面是它们可以快速地进行数组运算，而不直接需要循环。要做到这一点，维度需要直接对齐，这样它们才能成为广播。同样，这是通过约定和代码文档来完成的，这使得排列维度变得“容易”。例如，假设我们想要对上面的图像应用蒙版。
这是失败的，因为即使我们知道我们正在建造一个高度和宽度形状的面具，广播规则也没有正确的语义。要实现这一点，我们鼓励您使用视图或压缩我最不喜欢的函数。
#任一掩码=掩码。解压(-1)#或MASK=MASK。视图(96，96，1)#高度x宽度x通道IMS。MASTED_FILL(MASK，1)[0]。
注意，我们不需要对最左边的维度执行此操作，因此这里有一点抽象。然而，通过实际代码阅读时，数十个右侧视图和压缩内容会变得完全不可读。
您可能会看到前两个问题，认为只要小心，这些问题就会被运行时错误捕获。但是，即使很好地使用广播和索引的组合，也会导致很难捕获的问题。
A=IMS[1]。Mean(2，Keepdim=True)#高x宽x 1#(中间有很多代码)#...。#解释应该发生什么的代码注释。Dim=1b=a+ims。Mean(Dim，Keepdim=True)[0]#(或者可能应该是2？或0？)。索引=2b=a+ims。Mean(Dim，Keep Dim=True)[0]b。
在这里，我们假设编码器正在尝试使用缩减操作和维度索引来组合两个张量。(老实说，在这一点上，我已经忘记了维度代表什么)。
不过，主要的一点是，无论给定dim的值是什么，此代码都可以正常运行。这里的注释可能描述了正在发生的事情，但是代码本身不会抛出运行时错误。
基于这些问题，我认为深度学习代码应该转向更好的中心对象。其中有几个建议。在这里，为了好玩，我将开发一个新的原型。我有以下目标。
为了试验这些想法，我构建了一个名为NamedTensor的库，目前它是特定于PyTorch的，但理论上类似的想法可以在其他框架中使用。代码可以在github.com/atherardnlp/namedtensor上找到。
库的核心是一个对象，它包装张量并为每个维度提供名称。在这里，我们简单地用维度名称包装给定的火炬张量。
名称的第一个好处是能够完全取代对Dimand轴样式参数的需要。例如，假设我们想要对每列进行排序。
所提供的名称也为广播操作提供了基础，当两个命名张量之间存在二进制操作时，它们首先确保所有维度在名称上匹配，然后应用标准广播。为了演示，让我们返回到上面的掩码示例。在这里，我们只需声明我们的面具尺寸的名称，并要求图书馆计算出广播。
IM=命名张量(IMS[0]，(&#34；高度&#34；，&#34；宽度&#34；，&#34；通道&#34；)IM2=命名张量(IMS[1]，(&#34；高度&#34；，&#34；宽度&#34；，&#34；通道&#34；))掩码=命名张量(火炬。Randint(0，2，[96，96])。Byte()，(&#34；高度&#34；，&#34；宽度&#34；))im。MASTED_FILL(掩码，1)。
一个更普遍的特征是名称传感器之间张量收缩的点法。张量压缩是Einsum背后的机制，它是考虑点积、矩阵向量乘积、矩阵矩阵乘积等的推广的一种优雅的方法。
类似的符号可以用于稀疏索引(灵感来自einindex库)。这是有用的前嵌入查找和其他稀疏操作。
在幕后，所有命名的张量都充当张量对象。正如诸如此类的事情一样，维度的秩序和步幅确实很重要。TRANSPOSE和VIEW等操作对于维护这一点至关重要，但不幸的是它们很容易出错。
取而代之的是，考虑一种领域特定的语言转换，它大量借鉴了Alex Rogozhnikov优秀的einops包。
张量=命名张量(IMS，(&#39；b&39；，&#39；h&#39；，&#39；w&#39；，&#39；c&#39；))张量。Split(h=(&#39；h1&#39；，&#39；h2&#39；)，h2=2)。Split(w=(&#39；w1&#39；，&#39；w2&#39；)，w2=2)\。平均值(&#39；h2&#39；，&#39；w2&#39；))。堆栈(bw=(&#39；b&#39；，&#39；w1&#39；))。
张量=命名张量(IMS，(&#39；b&39；，&#39；h&#39；，&#39；w&#39；，&#39；c&#39；))张量。Split(b=(&#39；b1&#39；，&#39；b2&#39；)，b1=2)。平均(#39；c&39；)\。堆栈(bw=(&#34；b1&#34；，&#34；w&#34；)，bh=(&#39；b2&#39；，&#39；h&#39；))。转置(&#39；bh&#39；，&#39；bw&#39；)
从Torch中拉出了一些有用的命名替代函数。例如，解除绑定会将一个维分离为一个元组。
最后，窄索引可以用来代替花哨的索引。但是，您必须给出一个新的暗淡的名称(因为它不能再广播了)。
最后，命名张量试图让您直接隐藏不应该被内部函数访问的维度。函数MASK_TO将保持在保护任何较早维度不受函数操作的左侧掩码周围。最简单的示例使用掩码删除批次维度。
Def BAD_Function(x，y)：#访问私有批处理维度返回x。平均值(#34；批次#34；)x=ntorch。随机n(dict(批次=10，高度=100，宽度=100))y=ntorch.。RANDN(dict(Batch=10，Height=100，Width=100))try：BAD_Function(x.。MASK_TO(&#34；批&#34；)，y)除运行错误外错误为e：error=&#34；收到错误：&#34；+str(E)错误。
这是弱动态检查，可以通过内部功能关闭。在将来的版本中，也许我们可以添加函数注释来提升未命名的函数，以尊重这些属性。
为了说明为什么这些选择会带来更好的封装属性，让我们考虑一个真实世界的深度学习示例。
这个例子是我的同事Tim Rocktashel在博客PostDescription Einsum(https://rockt.github.io/2018/04/30/einsum).)中提出的。Tim的代码被建议作为原始PyTorch的更好替代方案。虽然我同意Einsum是向前迈进了一步，但它仍然落入了上述许多陷阱。
Def Random_ntensor(名称，Num=1，Requires_grad=false)：张量=[ntorch.。如果num==1，则返回张量[0]如果num==1，则返回张量类参数：def__init__(self，in_hid，out_hid)：Torch。MANUAL_SEED(0)SELF。怀伊，赛尔夫。什，赛尔夫。WR，赛尔夫。Wt=\Random_ntenors(dict(inhid=in_hid，outhid=out_hid)，num=4，requires_grad=True)self。BM，赛尔夫。布莱尔，赛尔夫。W=\Random_n张量(dict(outhid=out_hid)，Num=3，Requires_grad=True)
#Einsum实现import torch.nn.function as F def einsum_attn(params，Y，ht，rt1)：#--[BATCH_SIZE x HIDDEN_DIMENSION]tmp=torch。Einsum(&#34；ik，kl-&gt；il&34；，[ht，param.。WH。值])+\手电筒。Einsum(&#34；ik，kl-&gt；il&34；，[rt1，param.。WR.。值])mt=手电筒。Tanh(手电筒。Einsum(&#34；ijk，kl-&gt；ijl&34；，[Y，param.。怀俄明州。值])+\tmp。解压(1)。展开_AS(Y)+PARAMS。宾夕法尼亚州立大学。值)#--[BATCH_SIZE x SEQUENCE_LENGTH]at=F。Softmax(手电筒。Einsum(&#34；ijk，k-&gt；ij&34；，[mt，param.。W.。值])，DIM=-1)#--[BATCH_SIZE x HIDDED_DIMENSION]RT=手电筒。Einsum(&#34；ijk，ij-&gt；ik&34；，[Y，at])+\torch。Tanh(手电筒。Einsum(&#34；ij，jk-&gt；ik&34；，[rt1，param.。WT。值])+参数。Br.。值)#--[Batch_Size x Hidden_Dimension]，[Batch_Size x Sequence_Dimension]返回RT，位于。
此实现是对朴素的PyTorch实现的改进。它移动了许多视图和转置，这将是实现这一工作所必需的。但是，它仍然使用挤压，引用私有批处理DIM，并使用未强制执行的注释。
Def namedtensor_attn(参数，Y，ht，rt1)：tmp=ht。点(&#34；隐藏&#34；，参数。WH)+RT1。点(&#34；隐藏&#34；，参数。Wr)at=ntorch。Tanh(Y.。点(&#34；隐藏&#34；，参数。WY)+tmp+params。Bm)\。点(&#34；outhid&#34；，param.。W)\。Softmax(#34；seqlen&34；)rt=Y。点(&#34；序列&#34；，at)。堆栈(inhid=(&#39；outhid&#39；，))+\n手电筒。Tanh(RT1.。点(&#34；隐藏&#34；，参数。WT)+参数。Br)返回RT，位于。
(陷阱3)跨DIM的操作是明确的。例如，Softmax明显超出了序列。
#运行Einsum in_hid=7；out_hid=7 Y=手电筒。Randn(3，5，in_HID)ht，rt1=手电筒。Randn(3，in_HID)，火炬。Rann(3，in_hid)params=param(in_hid，out_hid)r，a=einsum_attn(params，Y，ht，rt1)。
#运行命名张量(隐藏批次)Y=命名张量(Y，(&#34；Batch&34；，&#34；seqlen&#34；，&#34；inHid&#34；)，MASK=1)ht=Named张量(ht，(&#34；Batch&#34；，&#34；inHID&#34；)，MASK=1)rt1=Named张量(RT1，(&#34；Batch&#34；，)，掩码=1)nr，na=namedtensor_attn(params，Y，ht，rt1)。
深度学习工具帮助研究人员实现标准模型，但它们也会影响研究人员的尝试。目前的模型可以用我们拥有的工具很好地构建，但是编程实践不会扩展到新的模型。
(例如，我们最近一直在研究的一个空间是离散潜变量模型，它通常有许多特定于问题的变量，每个变量都有各自的行变量维数。这一设置几乎立即打破了当前的张量范式。)
这篇博客文章只是这种方法可能走向何方的一个原型。如果你感兴趣，我希望能为这个图书馆的建设做出贡献。如果你想把公关寄给Namedtensor，我会给你一些建议。一些想法：
1)扩展到PyTorch之外：我们能以一种支持NumPy和TensorFlow的方式来推广这种方法吗？
2)与PyTorch模块交互：我们是否可以“提升”带有类型注释的PyTorch模块，以便我们知道它们是如何更改输入的？
3)错误检查：是否可以在函数中添加注释，给出前置条件和后置条件，以便自动检查尺寸。
如果下面有俗气的广告，很抱歉：Disqus似乎是自动做到这一点的。
